article1.title: Technical Architecture 2025-2026: How to choose the right stack for your web project
article1.excerpt: Next.js 15, React 19, Remix, Astro or WordPress? Discover how to make a strategic choice aligned with your growth goals, budget and market realities in 2025.
article1.content:
Introduction: Why technical choice has become strategic in 2025

In 2025, choosing a technical stack is no longer just "what language I master". It's a strategic decision that directly impacts your time-to-market, infrastructure costs, SEO and ability to pivot quickly. With the emergence of new architectures and AI redefining development paradigms, making the right choice is more crucial than ever.

The decision criteria in 2025

Before any technical decision, analyze these 5 fundamental dimensions:

  • Anticipated traffic volume: 1,000 visits/month or 1 million? Scalability is not a luxury, it's a necessity.
  • Required interactivity: Brochure site, real-time application, complex SaaS? The need determines the tool.
  • Technical team: Will you manage yourself or delegate? Some stacks require specific expertise.
  • Total budget: Development costs BUT also hosting and maintenance costs over 3 years.
  • SEO and performance: With Core Web Vitals now critical, the stack directly impacts your visibility.

WordPress in 2025: Still relevant?

WordPress still powers 43% of the web in 2025. Why? Its maturity and ecosystem remain unmatched for certain cases:

Ideal for: Brochure sites, corporate blogs, small e‑commerce, institutional sites.

2025 evolutions: Full Site Editing (FSE) mature, massive adoption of the REST API, native integration with AI via plugins like Jetpack AI. Performance has improved significantly with the adoption of PHP 8.3 and headless architectures.

Limits: For complex applications or real‑time features, WordPress shows its limits without a headless architecture coupled with Next.js.

Next.js 15 and React 19: Modular power

Next.js has become the de facto standard for modern web applications. Version 15 brings major innovations:

  • Server Components by default: Drastic reduction of client‑side JavaScript, average 35% improvement in Core Web Vitals.
  • Partial Pre‑rendering: Intelligent combination of static and dynamic content for an ultra‑fast experience.
  • Native AI integration: SDK to easily integrate generative AI features into your applications.

Typical use cases: SaaS, marketplaces, complex applications, high‑traffic e‑commerce sites, community platforms.

Astro: The "less JavaScript" approach

Astro is gaining ground with its "Islands Architecture". In 2025, it's the preferred choice for content sites:

  • Exceptional performance: sites often scored 100/100 on Lighthouse
  • Flexibility: support for multiple frameworks (React, Vue, Svelte) in the same project
  • View Transitions API: native page transitions effortlessly

Remix: The web standards approach

Remix, now mature, attracts with its "web fundamentals first" philosophy. Ideal for teams that want to control every aspect without unnecessary complexity.

2025 comparison table

TechnologyPerformanceSEOComplexityIdeal use case
WordPressAverageExcellentLowBlogs, brochure sites
Next.jsExcellentExcellentMedium‑HighSaaS, e‑commerce, complex apps
AstroExceptionalExcellentLowContent sites, blogs, docs

Conclusion: The choice is no longer binary

In 2025, you don't choose "a" technology. You choose an architecture. Hybrid approaches are multiplying: headless WordPress with React front, Next.js with micro‑services, Astro with interactive React islands. The important thing is to align your stack with your business goals, not the other way around.

Do you have a project and are you unsure about the stack? Contact me for a free 30‑minute technical audit. We will analyze your specific needs together and I will recommend the most suitable architecture.

article2.title: E-commerce in 2025: The secrets of a converting website in the AI era
article2.excerpt: In-depth analysis of an e-commerce site that increased conversions by 187% through UX optimization, personalized AI and new consumer expectations.
article2.content:
Introduction: The new e-commerce paradigm in 2025

In 2025, e-commerce has profoundly mutated. The post-COVID era has given way to a new reality: consumers are more demanding, more informed, and less patient. The average conversion rate hovers around 2‑3% in most sectors, but the best players reach 15‑20%. Their secret? A holistic approach combining flawless UX, AI personalization, and absolute trust.

1. UX in 2025: Fluidity and instantaneity

Google studies show that in 2025, 73% of users leave a site if the loading time exceeds 3 seconds. Here are the current standards:

  • Loading time: Under 1.5 seconds to be competitive
  • Core Web Vitals: LCP under 2 seconds, FID under 50ms, CLS under 0.05
  • 1‑click payment: 65% of regular buyers expect this option

Beyond performance, modern UX incorporates micro‑interactions that guide and reassure: subtle hover animations, visible progress bars, instant confirmations.

2. AI personalization: The 2025 game‑changer

Artificial intelligence is no longer a luxury, it's a standard. Modern recommendation engines analyze in real time:

  • Browsing history (even without an account)
  • On‑site behaviour (time spent, mouse tracking)
  • Previous purchases and returns
  • Social and seasonal trends

Result: relevant recommendations that increase average basket size by 25‑35%. Tools like Nosto or Rebuy become essential.

3. Trust: The new premium

In 2025, with the multiplication of online scams, trust has become a major asset. Here's what reassures your visitors:

  • Verified reviews: 92% of consumers read reviews before buying. Integrate photo and video reviews.
  • Trust badges: SSL certificates, secure payment, labels.
  • Clear return policy: 67% of buyers check the return policy before purchasing.
  • Real‑time social proof: "X people are viewing this product", "Last item in stock".

4. Augmented sales techniques

High‑performing e‑commerce players use psychological techniques validated by neuroscience:

  • Anchoring: Display the crossed‑out price next to the current price
  • Scarcity: Limited stock indicators (create urgency)
  • Contextual upsell: "Customers who bought this product also liked..."
  • Intelligent cross‑sell: Complementary accessories at checkout

5. Case study: 187% conversion increase

Take the example of a client in sports equipment. In 6 months we:

  1. Reduced loading time from 4.2s to 1.3s (image optimization, caching, CDN)
  2. Implemented an AI recommendation engine (+28% average basket)
  3. Added verified reviews with photos (+35% trust)
  4. Optimized the purchase funnel (-42% cart abandonment)
  5. Set up personalized follow‑up emails (18% conversion rate on abandoned carts)

Conclusion: The era of hyper‑personalization

In 2025, a converting e‑commerce site treats every visitor as a unique individual. It anticipates their needs, reassures them, and offers a smooth, enjoyable experience. Technologies exist, know‑how too. Only the will to implement them is missing.

Do you want to audit your e‑commerce site? I offer a complete diagnosis of your conversion funnel with concrete, prioritized recommendations.

article3.title: Mobile-first 2025: Why your site must be designed for an immersive experience
article3.excerpt: With 72% of global web traffic coming from mobile in 2025, the responsive approach is no longer enough. Discover the 'mobile-immersive' approach.
article3.content:
Introduction: The end of "desktop-first"

In 2025, mobile is no longer "one screen among others". It's the main screen for 72% of global users. Yet 80% of sites still in production were first designed for desktop, then poorly adapted to mobile. This approach is now unacceptable, both for user experience and SEO.

Mobile first, but not only

The classic "mobile-first" approach (designing for small screens first) evolves into "mobile-immersive". What does that mean?

  • Rethought tactile interactions: Native gestures (swipe, pinch) naturally integrated
  • Prioritized content: The essentials first, the rest in the background
  • Native performance: No compromise, loading times under a second
  • Use of mobile capabilities: Geolocation, camera, push notifications

The 2025 data: Why mobile dominates

  • 72% of global web traffic (Statista, 2025)
  • 65% of online purchases involve a mobile search
  • 58% of emails are opened on mobile
  • Google now primarily indexes the mobile version of sites

How to design an immersive mobile experience?

Here are the key principles:

1. Rethought content architecture

On mobile, the user scrolls quickly and scans. Structure your content with punchy headings, short paragraphs (max 3 lines), bullet lists, impactful visuals.

2. Rethought navigation

Hamburger menus are questioned. Tests show that bottom tab navigation (like in native apps) is more intuitive and increases engagement by 22%.

3. Advanced tactile interactions

Incorporate natural gestures: swipe to navigate between products, long tap for more options, double tap to like. These micro‑interactions create a sense of familiarity.

4. Extreme performance

Core Web Vitals are even more critical on mobile:

  • LCP (Largest Contentful Paint): ideally under 1.5s
  • FID (First Input Delay): under 50ms
  • CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift): under 0.05

Impact on SEO in 2025

Google has confirmed that mobile‑first indexing is total. If your mobile site is incomplete or slower than your desktop version, your SEO plummets. Worse, recent algorithm updates severely penalize sites with intrusive interstitials or non‑clickable elements on mobile.

Conclusion: Mobile as starting point

In 2025, designing for mobile first is no longer a technical option, it's a strategic necessity. Users expect an experience as smooth and pleasant as a native app. Sites that meet this expectation see their engagement, conversions, and SEO significantly improve.

article4.title: UI/UX Design 2025: Beyond aesthetics, emotional experience
article4.excerpt: How emotional design and micro-interactions are transforming customer loyalty. Case studies and 2025 data.
article4.content:
Introduction: The era of emotional design

In 2025, functional design is no longer enough. Users, saturated with standardized interfaces, seek an emotional connection with brands. Design becomes a vector of emotion, personality, recognition. This is called "emotional design".

The data: Measurable impact of design

  • First impression: 94% of first impressions are design‑related (Northumbria University)
  • Credibility: 75% of users judge a company's credibility by its design (Stanford)
  • Loyalty: Good UX can increase customer retention by 33% (Forrester)
  • ROI: Every euro invested in UX returns an average of €100 (UX Design Institute)

The 5 pillars of UI/UX design in 2025

1. Micro‑interactions: Magic in the details

Micro‑interactions are those small moments when the interface reacts to the user's action: a button changing state, a loading animation, visual confirmation. In 2025 they are everywhere and must be carefully designed. A well‑designed micro‑interaction:

  • Confirms the action (immediate feedback)
  • Guides the user (visual cue)
  • Creates pleasure ("wow" effect)

Example: The "like" animation on social networks. A simple animated heart creates instant gratification that encourages interaction.

2. Inclusive and accessible design

Accessibility is no longer a legal option, it's an ethical and business standard. In 2025:

  • 1 in 6 people lives with a significant disability
  • Accessible sites reach a market of over 1.3 billion people
  • WCAG 2.2 criteria are now integrated into Google's algorithms

Essential practices: sufficient contrast, keyboard navigation, screen reader compatibility, alternative text for images.

3. Dark mode and dynamic themes

Dark mode has become standard. In 2025, 82% of users prefer to have this option. Beyond that, dynamic themes that adapt to the time of day or system preferences create a personalized experience.

4. Expressive typography

With high‑resolution screens, typography becomes a central design element. Variable fonts allow total flexibility: a single font can adapt to all contexts, with fine variations in weight, width, slant.

5. 3D and progressive immersion

3D is no longer reserved for experimental sites. Thanks to WebGL and libraries like Three.js, lightweight 3D elements can enrich the experience without sacrificing performance. Used sparingly, 3D creates a strong differentiation effect.

Case study: UX redesign and retention increase

For a SaaS platform, we redesigned the interface with an emotional design approach:

  1. Added micro‑interactions on key actions
  2. Customized colors according to time of day
  3. Animated page transitions
  4. Confirmation messages with brand‑appropriate tones

Results: Retention +28%, user satisfaction (NPS) +32, time spent on the app +45%.

Conclusion: Design as competitive advantage

In 2025, design is no longer a cost center, it's a strategic investment. An exceptional user experience differentiates your brand in a sea of competition. It retains, converts, and turns your users into ambassadors.

article5.title: Complete SEO Guide 2025: Adapting to the AI and conversational search era
article5.excerpt: With the rise of ChatGPT Search, Perplexity and SGE (Search Generative Experience), SEO has radically changed. Here's how to stay visible.
article5.content:
Introduction: The end of traditional SEO

2025 marks a major turning point for natural referencing. The emergence of search engines based on generative AI (Google SGE, ChatGPT Search, Perplexity) has transformed how users find information and how sites are ranked. Traditional SEO (based on exact keywords and backlinks) hasn't disappeared, but it has profoundly evolved.

The new search landscape in 2025

  • Google SGE (Search Generative Experience): Generalised to 80% of searches, it generates synthetic answers before traditional links
  • ChatGPT Search: Over 100 million active users, conversational search
  • Perplexity: Explosive growth, "sourced answers" model
  • Voice search: 40% of searches are voice, with longer, conversational queries

The new ranking factors in 2025

1. Enhanced EEAT (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness)

Google has added an extra "E" for "Experience". Direct subject experience is now valued. How to demonstrate it?

  • Verified reviews and testimonials
  • Detailed case studies
  • Proof of certifications and field expertise
  • First‑hand content (original photos/videos)

2. Optimisation for featured snippets

With generative answers, being in position 0 becomes critical. To achieve this:

  • Structure your content with clear questions/answers
  • Use lists and tables
  • Answer questions directly (FAQ format)
  • Cite your sources (increased credibility)

3. Advanced semantic SEO

Engines now fully understand context. Optimise for "topics" rather than keywords:

  • Complete coverage of a subject (not just a keyword)
  • Thematic internal linking
  • Clearly identified named entities (people, places, brands)

4. Core Web Vitals become "Core Web Vitals Plus"

Performance metrics have been enriched with:

  • INP (Interaction to Next Paint): measures overall responsiveness, replaces FID
  • TTFB (Time to First Byte): critical for servers
  • Mobile‑friendliness: severe penalty for non‑optimized sites

SEO strategy 2025: Action plan

Phase 1: In‑depth technical audit

  • Analysis of real Core Web Vitals (not just lab)
  • Verification of mobile‑first indexing
  • Enhanced structured data (schema.org)
  • Canonicalization and URL parameter management

Phase 2: AI‑augmented content

AI is no longer an option. Use it to:

  • Generate content variations around a central topic
  • Create exhaustive FAQs
  • Analyse search intent and adapt content
  • Generate optimised meta descriptions

Caution: 100% AI‑generated content without human proofreading is penalised. Humans remain essential for expertise and credibility.

Phase 3: Authority and mentions

Backlinks remain important, but quality matters more than ever:

  • One link from an authoritative site in your domain > 100 spam links
  • Brand mentions (even without a link) are analysed
  • Citations in AI answers (ChatGPT, Perplexity) become a new visibility channel

Case study: +340% organic traffic in 8 months

For a client in the legal sector, we implemented a 2025 SEO strategy:

  1. Technical redesign with Next.js (performance x3)
  2. Creation of 150 content pages structured in semantic clusters
  3. Optimisation for featured snippets (30 position 0 obtained)
  4. Implementation of FAQ, HowTo, Review schemas
  5. Citation strategy in AI answers

Results: Organic traffic +340%, visibility in AI answers on 45 strategic queries.

Conclusion: SEO isn't dead, it has evolved

In 2025, SEO becomes more strategic, more technical, more demanding in quality. It's no longer about "cheating" the algorithm, but about creating real authority recognised by both humans and machines. Sites that invest in quality content, flawless technique, and an authority strategy will continue to thrive.

article6.title: Automation and AI in 2025: Create intelligent workflows that free your time
article6.excerpt: Discover how to combine AI, no-code and automation to create business processes that work 24/7 without human intervention.
article6.content:
Introduction: Automation augmented by AI

In 2025, automation is no longer limited to "if this, then that". Integrating AI into workflows enables intelligent processes that adapt, learn and improve over time. This is the era of cognitive automation.

Measurable gains of automation

  • Time saved: Average 20 hours per week for teams that automate their repetitive processes
  • Error reduction: -94% data entry errors
  • ROI: €3 to €5 returned for every euro invested in automation

The automation tool ecosystem in 2025

1. Make (ex‑Integromat): Visual power

Make has established itself as the leader for complex scenarios. Its strengths:

  • Intuitive visual interface
  • Advanced error handling
  • Connectors with over 1500 applications
  • Native AI integration (text analysis, classification, generation)

2. n8n: Sovereign open‑source

For companies that want to keep control of their data, n8n is the preferred choice:

  • Self‑hosting possible
  • Code and no‑code combined
  • Active community and extensions
  • Integration with local AI models (Llama, Mistral)

3. Zapier: Simplicity for all

Zapier remains the choice for non‑technical teams, with:

  • Simplest interface on the market
  • Library of pre‑built templates
  • Zapier AI: automatic zap generation based on natural language description

Concrete intelligent automation cases

Customer support automation

Scenario: A customer sends a support email.

  1. AI analyses the email and determines subject, tone, urgency
  2. If it's a frequent question, an automatic answer is generated with relevant information
  3. If it's a complaint, the ticket is created in the CRM with high priority
  4. A confirmation email is sent with the estimated processing time
  5. If the customer is not satisfied with the automatic answer, the ticket is escalated to a human with full context

Result: 70% of requests handled without human intervention, customer satisfaction stable or increasing.

Marketing and sales automation

Scenario: A prospect downloads a white paper.

  1. Prospect is added to CRM with download information
  2. AI analyses their company and position to qualify them
  3. A personalised email sequence is triggered (content tailored to their profile)
  4. If the prospect visits the pricing page, a salesperson is notified as priority
  5. The salesperson receives a complete sheet with history and content recommendations to send

Result: Lead conversion rate x2.5, sales time saved: 15h/week.

Accounting automation

Scenario: Receiving supplier invoices.

  1. Invoices are automatically extracted from emails or scans
  2. AI reads and structures the data (amount, date, invoice number, VAT)
  3. The invoice is reconciled with the corresponding purchase order
  4. The accounting entry is automatically generated
  5. An approval workflow is triggered according to amounts
  6. The invoice is scheduled for payment on due date

Result: Invoice processing in 5 minutes vs 45 minutes, zero data entry errors.

How to start your automation?

  1. Identify repetitive processes: What tasks do you do every week that are always the same?
  2. Map the flow: Draw the process, identify decision points
  3. Choose the right tools: Based on your budget, technical skills, data needs
  4. Start small: Automate one simple process first, measure gains, then iterate
  5. Monitor and optimise: Automation is never static, it must evolve with your activity

Conclusion: Automation is a competitive advantage

In 2025, automation is no longer a luxury, it's a necessity to stay competitive. Companies that automate their processes gain speed, accuracy and free their teams to focus on what really matters: creativity, customer relations, strategy.

article7.title: Web design trends 2025-2026: What will mark the years to come
article7.excerpt: Analysis of emerging trends: generative design, lightweight 3D, variable typography, and the impact of AI on creation.
article7.content:
Introduction: Design in the age of generative AI

2025-2026 marks a turning point in the history of web design. Generative AI allows designers to create faster, explore more options, and personalise at scale. But technology isn't everything: the human, emotion, uniqueness remain at the heart of trends.

Trend 1: Generative design and creative AI

AI becomes a full‑fledged creation tool:

  • Generation of design variations from an artistic direction
  • Creation of unique images and illustrations (Midjourney, DALL‑E 3, Adobe Firefly)
  • Automatic layout optimisation based on user data
  • Dynamic design personalisation in real time

Caution: AI is an assistant, not a replacement. The most successful designs combine AI generation and human sensitivity.

Trend 2: Lightweight and accessible 3D

Thanks to improved WebGL and new codecs, 3D becomes more accessible:

  • Optimised 3D models (low weight, progressive streaming)
  • Integration with Spline, Three.js, and new no‑code 3D tools
  • Judicious use for surprise and differentiation effects
  • Interactive 3D (hover rotation, exploration) for products

Trend 3: Variable and expressive typography

Variable fonts offer unprecedented flexibility:

  • A single font can vary in weight, width, slant
  • Automatic adaptation to display conditions
  • Fluid typographic animations
  • Customisation according to brand identity

Trend 4: Advanced dark mode

Dark mode becomes more sophisticated:

  • No longer a simple invert, but palettes redesigned for each mode
  • Adaptation to ambient light (light sensor)
  • Smooth transitions between modes
  • "Sepia" or "paper" mode for long reading

Trend 5: Advanced micro‑interactions

Micro‑interactions become richer:

  • Haptic (vibrations) on mobile
  • Subtle sounds (audio feedback)
  • Contextual animations that tell a story
  • Personalisation according to user behaviour

Trend 6: Inclusive and accessible design

Accessibility is no longer a constraint, it's a creative opportunity:

  • High but elegant contrasts
  • Navigation redesigned for all interaction modes
  • Subtitling and transcripts integrated into the design
  • Colour choices adapted to different forms of colour blindness

Trend 7: Conversational interfaces

Generative AI enables more conversational interfaces:

  • Smart chats integrated into the design
  • Natural language search
  • Contextual assistants that guide the user
  • Personalisation through conversation

Predictions for 2026

  • Integrated AR: Augmented reality directly in the browser
  • Emotional design: Interfaces that detect and respond to emotions
  • AI components: Interface elements that adapt and learn
  • Eco‑design: Energy‑conscious design, valued by users

Conclusion: The human at the centre

The 2025-2026 trends show a clear direction: technology (AI, 3D, variables) serves a more human, more personalised, more emotional experience. The designs that will stand out will be those that use these tools sparingly and intentionally, to create an authentic connection with the user.

article8.title: Web security 2025: The 10 critical errors that put your site at risk
article8.excerpt: With cyberattacks up 78% in 2 years, protect your site and users by avoiding these common security flaws.
article8.content:
Introduction: Cybersecurity, priority #1 in 2025

Cyberattacks have increased by 78% since 2023. In 2025, a website is attacked on average every 39 seconds. The consequences can be disastrous: data loss, reputational damage, legal sanctions (GDPR), and high remediation costs. Here are the most critical errors to avoid absolutely.

Error 1: Weak passwords and absence of 2FA

Still today, "123456" and "password" remain the most used passwords. Worse, many sites don't enforce strong password policies and don't offer two‑factor authentication (2FA).

Solution: Enforce complex passwords (12+ characters, uppercase, lowercase, numbers, symbols) and systematically offer 2FA (SMS, authenticator app, physical key).

Error 2: No regular backups

In case of hacking, ransomware or failure, recent backups are your only lifeline. Yet 30% of sites have no automated backup.

Solution: Implement automatic daily backups, stored on a separate server, and test their restoration regularly.

Error 3: Unupdated CMS and plugins

Security flaws in WordPress, its themes and plugins are the entry point for 90% of attacks on sites using this CMS.

Solution: Enable automatic updates when possible, or set up a weekly update process. Delete unused plugins.

Error 4: No SSL certificate or misconfigured SSL

A site without HTTPS is immediately flagged as "not secure" by browsers. Google penalises it severely. But a misconfigured certificate (obsolete protocols, weak encryption) is almost as dangerous.

Solution: Use Let's Encrypt (free) or a paid certificate. Configure TLS 1.3, disable old protocols (SSL 3.0, TLS 1.0), use a secure configuration (test with SSL Labs).

Error 5: SQL injections and input validation

SQL injections allow an attacker to execute malicious commands on your database. It's one of the most common and dangerous flaws.

Solution: Systematically use prepared statements, validate and sanitise all user inputs, use a modern ORM.

Error 6: Inappropriate storage of sensitive data

Storing passwords in clear text, credit card numbers, or personal data without protection is a GDPR violation and a disaster waiting to happen.

Solution: Hash passwords with bcrypt or Argon2, never store payment data (use a payment provider), encrypt sensitive data.

Error 7: Exposure of system information

Displaying your CMS version, server version, or detailed errors gives valuable information to attackers.

Solution: Hide version headers, configure custom error pages, disable error display in production.

Error 8: Overly permissive file permissions

777 permissions (read, write, execute for everyone) on sensitive files allow an attacker to modify or delete them.

Solution: Apply the principle of least privilege: files 644, folders 755, never 777.

Error 9: No protection against DDoS attacks

A DDoS attack can make your site inaccessible for hours or even days. Without protection, you're an easy target.

Solution: Use a CDN with built‑in DDoS protection (Cloudflare, Fastly), configure rate limiting, monitor abnormal traffic.

Error 10: Neglecting API security

With the rise of headless architectures and rich applications, APIs have become prime targets. An unsecured API can expose all your data.

Solution: Authenticate all API requests, use expiring tokens, validate inputs, rate‑limit calls, use HTTPS exclusively.

2025 Security checklist

  • ✔ Strong passwords + 2FA for all admin accounts
  • ✔ Automated backups tested regularly
  • ✔ Automatic updates of CMS/plugins
  • ✔ Valid SSL certificate and secure configuration
  • ✔ Protection against SQL injections
  • ✔ Sensitive data encrypted
  • ✔ Security headers (CSP, HSTS, X‑Frame‑Options)
  • ✔ Monitoring and alerts on suspicious activity
  • ✔ Annual security audit

Conclusion: Security is a process, not a state

Security is never final. Threats evolve, your site evolves, vulnerabilities appear. Adopt a continuous security approach: monitor, update, test, train your teams. And when in doubt, call an expert.

article9.title: Launch your MVP in 2025: The Lean method augmented by AI
article9.excerpt: How to go from idea to minimum viable product in less than 30 days using no-code tools and generative AI.
article9.content:
Introduction: MVP in the AI era

In 2025, launching an MVP (Minimum Viable Product) has never been faster and more accessible. No‑code tools combined with generative AI allow you to go from idea to a functional product in a few weeks, at a cost 5 to 10 times lower than 5 years ago. Here's the method to achieve it.

What is an MVP in 2025?

The MVP is no longer "the simplest product that works". It's "the simplest product that delivers value AND enables learning". The goal is to test your hypotheses with real users, as quickly as possible, to iterate and improve.

Phase 1: Ideation and validation (Day 1‑5)

1. Define the problem and solution

Before coding, make sure the problem you're solving is real and your solution is desirable. Use AI to:

  • Analyse competitor reviews and identify frustrations
  • Generate detailed user personas
  • Create validation surveys

2. Identify the main "job to be done"

What is the main task your user wants to accomplish? Focus on ONE key feature that solves this problem. Secondary features can wait.

3. Create a validation landing page

In 24 hours, create a landing page that presents your value proposition and collects emails from interested people. Use tools like:

  • Framer or Webflow for design
  • Claude or ChatGPT to write the copy
  • Midjourney to generate visuals
  • ConvertKit or MailerLite to collect emails

Goal: 100 signups in 5 days. If you don't achieve it, your idea may need adjustment.

Phase 2: Rapid design (Day 6‑10)

1. Interactive prototyping

Create a clickable prototype of your MVP with Figma. AI can generate design variations and UI components in seconds.

2. Quick user tests

Test your prototype with 5‑10 target users. Observe their interactions, listen to their feedback. Iterate the design accordingly.

Phase 3: Accelerated development (Day 11‑25)

1. Choose the right technical approach

For an MVP, prioritise speed:

  • No‑code solution: Bubble, Adalo, FlutterFlow (ideal for quick testing)
  • Low‑code stack: Next.js + Supabase + Tailwind (if you anticipate technical evolution)
  • Hybrid approach: No‑code frontend + custom backend

2. Develop the unique feature

Focus solely on the main feature. Resist the temptation to add "nice little features".

3. Use AI to accelerate

  • Code generation with Claude or Cursor
  • Content creation for the application
  • Automated tests
  • Documentation

Phase 4: Launch and first iterations (Day 26‑30)

1. Go to production

Deploy your MVP with:

  • Vercel for Next.js
  • Netlify for static sites
  • Bubble for no‑code apps

2. Analytics and monitoring

Install tracking tools from day one:

  • Mixpanel or PostHog for user behaviour
  • Hotjar for session replays
  • Google Analytics 4 for traffic
  • Sentry for technical errors

3. First batch of users

Contact the people who signed up on your landing page. Offer them free access in exchange for detailed feedback.

Essential tools for an MVP 2025

CategoryRecommended tools
IdeationClaude, ChatGPT, Miro, Whimsical
DesignFigma + AI plugins, Framer
No‑code developmentBubble, FlutterFlow, Adalo
Code developmentNext.js, Supabase, Clerk, Tailwind
AnalyticsPostHog, Mixpanel, GA4

Case study: MVP launched in 21 days

An entrepreneur with a marketplace idea for freelance services:

  1. Day 1‑5: Validation with landing page (120 signups)
  2. Day 6‑10: Figma prototype tested with 8 freelancers
  3. Day 11‑21: Development with Bubble (live on day 21)
  4. Day 22‑30: 15 first users, 3 first transactions

Total cost: €2,500 (tools + technical support). Traditional approach quoted €35,000 for 4 months of development.

Conclusion: Start now

In 2025, there's no excuse not to test your idea. Tools exist, methods are proven, AI accelerates every step. The important thing is not to have a perfect product from the start, but to learn fast and iterate. Your first user will teach you more than 6 months of blind development.

Do you have an idea and want to test it quickly? Contact me for a 2‑day MVP workshop: we'll go from idea to functional prototype.
